tasks.description: Prioritization matrix data layer: migration adding effort_score and impact_score columns to steps and touchpoints tables, TypeScript type updates, API PATCH support for new columns
acceptance_criteria: New migration file 021_prioritization_scores.sql adds effort_score INT CHECK (1-5) nullable and impact_score INT CHECK (1-5) nullable to both steps AND touchpoints tables
Step interface in src/types/database.ts includes effort_score: number | null and impact_score: number | null
Touchpoint interface in src/types/database.ts includes effort_score: number | null and impact_score: number | null
Steps PATCH route (src/app/api/v1/steps/[id]/route.ts) includes effort_score and impact_score in EDITABLE_FIELDS
Touchpoints PATCH route (src/app/api/v1/touchpoints/[id]/route.ts) includes effort_score and impact_score in EDITABLE_FIELDS
npx tsc --noEmit passes cleanly after changes

patterns_to_follow: Migration 007 pattern: ALTER TABLE ... ADD COLUMN ... INT CHECK (col BETWEEN 1 AND 5) — nullable by default
Steps EDITABLE_FIELDS already includes maturity_score and target_maturity — add effort_score and impact_score to the same array
Touchpoints EDITABLE_FIELDS: add effort_score and impact_score to the existing array
database.ts Step interface: existing maturity_score: number | null pattern — add effort_score and impact_score with same type
learnings_relevant: NEXT_PUBLIC_ static replacement — not relevant for data layer
apiFetch unwraps envelope — existing pattern, follow it

tasks.special_instructions: Follow migration 007 pattern exactly. Two ALTER TABLE statements (one for steps, one for touchpoints). Each adds two nullable INT columns with CHECK constraint BETWEEN 1 AND 5. The Touchpoint interface currently has no maturity fields — add effort_score and impact_score after the position_y field, before created_at.

tasks.description: Clone confirmation dialog should display workspace name in the description text
acceptance_criteria: DialogDescription in the Duplicate Workspace dialog includes the workspace name dynamically
Text reads something like: 'A full copy of "[workspace name]" will be created including all tabs, sections, steps, connections, teams, roles, people, and tools.'
The settings page body text paragraph also includes the workspace name for consistency

patterns_to_follow: IMP-028 already replaced confirm() with Radix Dialog — modify the existing DialogDescription content
IMP-029 already updated body text — modify it to include workspace name
Workspace object is available in the settings page context (useWorkspace hook or server-side fetch)

tasks.special_instructions: The workspace object should already be available in the settings page. Find where the clone dialog is rendered (look for confirmCloneOpen state) and inject workspace.name into the DialogDescription string. Use template literal with quotes around the name.

tasks.description: Runbooks list progress bar should show step count text (e.g. '3 / 5') alongside the bar
acceptance_criteria: Each runbook card in the list view shows a text label like '3 / 5' next to or below the progress bar
Text uses existing text hierarchy (text-[var(--text-secondary)] or text-[var(--text-tertiary)])
Text shows resolved count (completed + skipped) out of total, matching playbook-view.tsx pattern

patterns_to_follow: IMP-014 already established resolvedCount = completedCount + skippedCount pattern in this file
playbook-view.tsx shows 'Step N of M' text — follow similar compact format for list cards
IMP-013 added the segmented progress bar — add text label adjacent to it

tasks.special_instructions: The runbooks-list-view already has completedCount, skippedCount, and total step counts computed per runbook card (from IMP-014). Add a small text label showing the fraction, matching the existing text sizing pattern in the card.

shared_files.note: database.ts is owned by slot 1. No other slot should modify it.
